summary_one_line: Input must encode a glyph whose USE shaping category resolves to H/HVM/IS (halant) and is non-ligated, reached by broad havoc exploration.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font/text input that drives at least one glyph into the USE shaper's halant category (use_category() == USE(H) || USE(HVM) || USE(IS)) while that glyph is NOT ligated, so the true branch at line 357 fires. The category is assigned by harfbuzz's USE machine-class tables from glyph properties, not by a literal integer/string CMP against an input byte field.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves 10/10 and value_profile resolves 10/10; their winner-resolving seeds are broad havoc/interesting-byte mutations (e.g. BitFlipMutator / DwordInterestingMutator / QwordAddMutator chains) that produce varied glyph-category inputs and hit the true side 18 and 15 times respectively.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 10/10 and value_profile_cmplog blocks 8/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch; their I2SRandReplace stage substitutes intercepted CMP operands into the input, steering bytes toward solved integer comparisons that do not control the shaper-derived USE category, so budget is diverted away from producing a halant-category glyph.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is enabled by plain havoc byte mutation (naive's havoc/token stack, also present unmodified in value_profile), not by any comparison-solving. The branch condition tests a shaper-derived glyph category, not a byte-equality roadblock, so the comparison-operand substitution added by the I2S-bearing fuzzers is counterproductive here and those fuzzers are the losers.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is a disjunctive equality test on the glyph's USE shaping category combined with a non-ligated requirement.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: return (info.use_category() == USE(H) || info.use_category() == USE(HVM) || info.use_category() == USE(IS)) &&

evidence_trail.claim: The true side of the branch is the globally blocked side, requiring the halant category to be satisfied.
naive resolves the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog blocks in all 10, with naive hitting the true side 18 times and cmplog 0 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=18  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolves the branch (10/10) while value_profile_cmplog blocks it (8/10) with the winner hitting the true side 15 times and the loser 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=15  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seeds were found by naive using broad havoc/interesting-byte mutation chains, not comparison substitution.
cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add the I2SRandReplace mutator that substitutes one CMP operand into input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive uses only the havoc + token mutation stack with no I2S stage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses naive's havoc+token stack with no I2SRandReplace, so its win is not comparison-solving.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is havoc-only (not I2SRandReplace), so lineage verification is not mandatory. The winners (naive, value_profile) carry no I2S stage at all per the MECHANISM CONTEXT section, so a dash-row lineage check is inapplicable to the winning side; the I2S-bearing fuzzers are the losers here, and the winner's seeds were found by broad havoc/interesting-byte chains (e.g. BitFlipMutator/DwordInterestingMutator).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a halant-class glyph category where cmplog/vpc resolve as fast as or faster than naive/value_profile would refute the claim that I2S substitution is counterproductive and that plain havoc is the enabling mechanism.

weakest_evidence_point: The seeds are font-shaper inputs whose bytes map to glyph categories through opaque USE machine-class tables, so I cannot directly tie any specific input offset in the BYTE DIFF to the halant category that satisfies the gate.
